description:
Goal: make source forms define modules, functions, macros, types, protocols, imports, aliases, and requires through compiler2-owned services that read Fz-shaped quoted source roots and publish existing fact families. Those services are available both to Rust source-production code and to compile-time macro IR through the `Fz.Compiler` interface.

Signal:
- literal source that does not use macros still publishes the same downstream facts as today: `ModuleIndexed`, `CodeScoped`, `ModuleDefined`, `FunctionDefined`, function-ast/source facts, `TypeDefined` waits, `ProtocolDispatch`, and function type-reference wait sets
- `def`, `defmodule`, `defmacro`, `@type`, `@spec`, `defprotocol`, and `defimpl` are handled through one source-production service boundary over Fz-shaped source readers/cursors
- `Fz.Compiler` service externs accept AST roots as ordinary Fz `AnyValueRef` values (`ExternTy::Any`); the active compile-time `Process` supplies the implied heap/world
- a macro can hand a function AST root back to the compiler service, and the compiler saves it as the function's source/AST fact with revision semantics that wake lower/contract/semantic jobs correctly
- downstream body/dispatch/semantic/artifact jobs do not learn whether a definition was literal source, returned by a macro, or submitted through `Fz.Compiler`
- no service implementation requires a repeated Fz-value -> Rust AST -> Fz-value conversion loop

Change:
- replace direct `define_scope` over `Rc<Item>` with source-production services over rooted Fz-shaped quoted source values
- centralize function/module/type/protocol definition publication so literal source, macro-produced source, and `Fz.Compiler` calls use the same path
- define the compile-time `Fz.Compiler` host interface: it receives `AnyValueRef` roots, reads them against the current process heap/source context, and publishes compiler2 facts
- persist function source/AST roots as owned compiler2 facts/state keyed by `FunctionId`; fact revisions must be driven by quoted-source identity, not by pointer-stable old AST structs
- preserve existing type-reference recording and contract/type-definition waits at the service boundary
- when a downstream consumer still needs a Rust struct, create the narrow internal record at the final fact boundary only, not during every expansion step
- keep protocol type facts and dispatch facts as facts, without folding protocol-domain cleanup from `fz-rh2.12.5` into this ticket

Why:
- Elixir-style `def` works because the compiler remains the authority; macros produce or submit source definitions, but compiler services store them
- this is the seam that keeps downstream compiler2 structurally unchanged while the expansion loop stays Fz-shaped
title: Compiler2 compiler services: publish facts from quoted source forms
